GH6-oracle-twap-window-cross-block-warp.name: Oracle TWAP window skew via cross-block timestamp warp
GH6-oracle-twap-window-cross-block-warp.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-oracle-twap-window-cross-block-warp.setup: Pure-logic simulation (no chain needed; faithful model of a Uniswap-V2-style cumulative-price TWAP). Save as twap_warp.py and run: python3 twap_warp.py . Models an oracle that derives a time-weighted average price from price0CumulativeLast snapshots taken at two block timestamps; an attacker who controls reserve mutation between snapshots and the (proposer-influenced) timestamp delta skews the average a consumer trusts.
GH6-oracle-twap-window-cross-block-warp.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH6 TWAP window cross-block warp -- AUTHORIZED LAB / OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# Faithful logic model of a V2-style cumulative-price TWAP oracle.
# Demonstrates the trust boundary: TWAP correctness assumes an honest, well-spaced
# observation window; a short manipulated window (proposer-warped timestamp + reserve
# nudge) lets the average diverge from the true long-run price.
# No chain, no funds, no keys. Pure arithmetic.
import sys

UQ = 2**112  # fixed-point scale used by V2 oracles

class Pair:
    def __init__(self, r0, r1, ts):
        self.r0, self.r1 = r0, r1
        self.price0_cum = 0
        self.last_ts = ts
    def _accumulate(self, now):
        dt = now - self.last_ts
        if dt > 0:
            # price0 = reserve1/reserve0 in UQ112.112, times elapsed seconds
            self.price0_cum += (self.r1 * UQ // self.r0) * dt
            self.last_ts = now
    def sync(self, r0, r1, now):
        self._accumulate(now)
        self.r0, self.r1 = r0, r1

def twap(cum_a, ts_a, cum_b, ts_b):
    return (cum_b - cum_a) / (ts_b - ts_a) / UQ

def main():
    # True long-run price ~ 1.0 (reserves balanced) over an honest 30-min window.
    p = Pair(1_000_000, 1_000_000, 0)
    p._accumulate(0)
    snap_a = (p.price0_cum, p.last_ts)
    # HONEST consumer expects ~1800s spacing.
    p._accumulate(1800)
    honest = twap(snap_a[0], snap_a[1], p.price0_cum, p.last_ts)
    print("HONEST window dt=1800s  twap=%.4f" % honest)

    # ATTACK: re-snapshot, then within a tiny warped window the attacker swings
    # reserves AND the proposer warps the block timestamp to a 2s delta so the
    # skewed spot price dominates the 'average'.
    p2 = Pair(1_000_000, 1_000_000, 0)
    p2._accumulate(0)
    a = (p2.price0_cum, p2.last_ts)
    p2.sync(200_000, 1_800_000, 0)        # attacker pushes price0 to ~9.0
    p2._accumulate(2)                      # WARP: only 2s elapse before consumer reads
    skewed = twap(a[0], a[1], p2.price0_cum, p2.last_ts)
    print("WARPED window dt=2s     twap=%.4f" % skewed)

    drift = skewed / honest
    print("drift factor = %.2fx" % drift)
    if drift > 3.0:
        print("[SEAM CONFIRMED] short warped observation window skews TWAP >3x vs honest window")
        sys.exit(0)
    print("[no skew]"); sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
GH6-oracle-twap-window-cross-block-warp.success_criterion: stdout prints HONEST twap ~1.0, WARPED twap ~9.x, a drift factor >3x, and '[SEAM CONFIRMED]' with exit 0 -- proving an attacker-controlled (short) observation window lets the TWAP an oracle consumer trusts diverge sharply from the true long-run price.
GH6-oracle-twap-window-cross-block-warp.cleanup: rm -f twap_warp.py (no node started). If you ran the optional anvil variant: kill the anvil PID.
GH6-oracle-twap-window-cross-block-warp.safety: Local-only pure arithmetic simulation; no chain, no funds, no keys, models only the math of the trust boundary.

GH6-bridge-lightclient-finality-reorg-mi.name: Bridge light-client mints on sub-finality header before reorg
GH6-bridge-lightclient-finality-reorg-mi.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-bridge-lightclient-finality-reorg-mi.setup: Pure-logic simulation of a cross-chain bridge's light-client header verifier + mint queue. Save as bridge_reorg.py and run: python3 bridge_reorg.py . Models a source-chain header chain with a finality depth requirement; the bridge mints destination tokens after seeing a header at depth < required_finality, then the source chain reorgs that header away.
GH6-bridge-lightclient-finality-reorg-mi.success_criterion: stdout shows 'MINT issued ... at depth=2', then 'post-reorg depth ... = -1 (orphaned)', then '[SEAM CONFIRMED]' with exit 0 -- proving the bridge minted destination tokens backed by a source header that the reorg removed, i.e. an unbacked mint when finality depth is not enforced.
GH6-bridge-lightclient-finality-reorg-mi.safety: Local-only simulation of header-chain logic; no real bridge, RPC, funds, or keys involved.

GH6-proxy-upgrade-storage-collision-sile.name: Proxy upgrade storage collision -> silent slot reinterpretation
GH6-proxy-upgrade-storage-collision-sile.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-proxy-upgrade-storage-collision-sile.setup: Foundry + local anvil ONLY. mkdir collision && cd collision && forge init --no-git . ; place this file at test/Collision.t.sol ; run: anvil & ; forge test -vv (Foundry runs its own in-process EVM; no mainnet). A minimal ERC1967-style proxy delegatecalls an implementation. V1 lays out [owner, balance]; V2 inserts a new var first, shifting the layout so the slot that held `owner` is now read as `paused`.
GH6-proxy-upgrade-storage-collision-sile.success_criterion: forge test -vv passes testSilentReslot and logs 'V2 paused (slot0 reinterpreted)=1' and 'V2 owner (slot1 reinterpreted)=0x...0309' (decimal 777, the old balance), then '[SEAM CONFIRMED]' -- proving an upgrade with a shifted layout silently reinterpreted existing storage (paused flipped, owner corrupted) with no revert or event.
GH6-proxy-upgrade-storage-collision-sile.cleanup: kill the anvil process (e.g. pkill -f anvil); rm -rf collision/ to remove the scratch project.
GH6-proxy-upgrade-storage-collision-sile.safety: Local-only: Foundry's in-process EVM / local anvil, defanged 0xEXAMPLE owner, no mainnet, no funds, no real keys.

GH6-eip712-domain-replay-fork-chainid-co.name: EIP-712 permit replay across chainId / redeployed verifier
GH6-eip712-domain-replay-fork-chainid-co.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-eip712-domain-replay-fork-chainid-co.setup: Node + ethers v6, offline. npm i ethers@6 ; save as eip712_replay.js ; run: node eip712_replay.js . No chain/RPC needed -- it signs an EIP-712 'Permit' with a throwaway test key bound to domain {chainId:1, verifyingContract:V}, then runs a verifier that (the seam) hardcodes/ caches the domain separator for chainId 1 and so still accepts the same signature when the effective chainId is 137 (post-fork) or the contract is redeployed.
GH6-eip712-domain-replay-fork-chainid-co.success_criterion: stdout prints 'orig chain (1) accepts: true', 'forked chain cached-domain accepts: true | correct(137) accepts: false', then '[SEAM CONFIRMED]' with exit 0 -- proving the cached/hardcoded EIP-712 domain separator lets one signature be replayed after a chainId fork (or verifier redeploy), whereas a correct live-chainId domain rejects it.
GH6-eip712-domain-replay-fork-chainid-co.cleanup: rm -f eip712_replay.js ; optionally rm -rf node_modules (no chain started).
GH6-eip712-domain-replay-fork-chainid-co.safety: Offline ethers signing with well-known throwaway dev test keys and defanged 0xEXAMPLE/placeholder addresses; no RPC, no mainnet, no funds.

GH6-blind-signing-simulation-divergence-.name: Blind-signing: simulated tx result diverges from execution
GH6-blind-signing-simulation-divergence-.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-blind-signing-simulation-divergence-.setup: Pure-logic simulation of a wallet 'simulate then sign then mine' flow. Save as blind_sign.py and run: python3 blind_sign.py . Models contract state read at simulation time vs. an adversary-induced state change (e.g., approval/route swap or front-run) that lands between the user's simulation and the tx being mined, so the user-approved 'preview' (decoy) does not match the executed effect.
GH6-blind-signing-simulation-divergence-.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH6 blind-signing simulation/execution divergence (decoy preview) -- LAB/OFFLINE SIM.
# Trust boundary: wallets show users a SIMULATED outcome to approve, then sign blindly.
# If shared state mutates between simulation and inclusion, the executed effect differs
# from the previewed (decoy) one. No chain, funds, or keys -- models the ordering gap.
import sys

class Vault:
    def __init__(self):
        self.rate = 1.0           # tokens-out per token-in
        self.recipient = "user"
    def quote(self, amount_in):   # what simulation reads
        return amount_in * self.rate, self.recipient
    def execute(self, amount_in): # what actually runs at mine time
        return amount_in * self.rate, self.recipient

def main():
    v = Vault()
    amount = 100.0

    # 1) User simulates -> wallet shows this preview and asks for a blind signature.
    sim_out, sim_to = v.quote(amount)
    print("PREVIEW (simulated): out=%.1f to=%s" % (sim_out, sim_to))
    user_approves = (sim_out >= 95.0 and sim_to == "user")
    print("user approves blind signature:", user_approves)

    # 2) THE SEAM: between sim and inclusion, an attacker (mempool/owner/route) mutates
    #    the shared state the tx will touch -- the signed tx still goes through.
    v.rate = 0.10
    v.recipient = "attacker"

    # 3) Tx is mined against the NEW state.
    exec_out, exec_to = v.execute(amount)
    print("EXECUTED: out=%.1f to=%s" % (exec_out, exec_to))

    diverged = (abs(exec_out - sim_out) > 1.0) or (exec_to != sim_to)
    if user_approves and diverged:
        print("[SEAM CONFIRMED] executed result diverges from the simulated preview the user approved")
        sys.exit(0)
    print("[consistent]"); sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
GH6-blind-signing-simulation-divergence-.success_criterion: stdout shows 'PREVIEW (simulated): out=100.0 to=user', 'user approves ... True', 'EXECUTED: out=10.0 to=attacker', then '[SEAM CONFIRMED]' with exit 0 -- proving the result the user blind-signed against (the preview) is not the result executed once shared state changed between simulation and inclusion.
GH6-blind-signing-simulation-divergence-.safety: Local-only simulation of the simulate/sign/mine ordering; no chain, mempool, funds, or keys touched.

GH6-did-credential-status-staleness-trus.name: DID/VC verifier honors cached status after revocation
GH6-did-credential-status-staleness-trus.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-did-credential-status-staleness-trus.setup: Offline simulation of a Verifiable Credential verifier with a status-list cache (W3C StatusList2021-style). Save as did_staleness.py and run: python3 did_staleness.py . Models an issuer revoking a credential (flipping its status-list bit) while the verifier serves a TTL-cached status list, so a revoked credential still verifies as valid.
GH6-did-credential-status-staleness-trus.script:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# GH6 DID/VC credential-status staleness -- AUTHORIZED LAB / OFFLINE SIM ONLY.
# Trust boundary: a verifier must check LIVE revocation status. A verifier that trusts
# a TTL-cached status list keeps accepting a credential after the issuer revokes it.
# No keys/chain -- models the status-list bit + cache.
import sys, time

class StatusList:
    def __init__(self, size=64):
        self.bits = [0]*size           # 0 = valid, 1 = revoked
        self.version = 1
    def revoke(self, idx):
        self.bits[idx] = 1
        self.version += 1

class Verifier:
    def __init__(self, issuer_list, ttl=3600):
        self.issuer = issuer_list
        self.ttl = ttl
        self.cache = None
        self.cache_at = -1
    def _status_list(self, now):
        # THE SEAM: refresh only when TTL expires; otherwise serve stale snapshot.
        if self.cache is None or now - self.cache_at >= self.ttl:
            self.cache = list(self.issuer.bits)
            self.cache_at = now
        return self.cache
    def verify(self, cred_idx, now):
        revoked = self._status_list(now)[cred_idx] == 1
        return (not revoked)

def main():
    issuer = StatusList()
    idx = 7
    v = Verifier(issuer, ttl=3600)

    now = 1000
    print("t=%d verify (pre-revoke):" % now, v.verify(idx, now))   # caches valid

    # Issuer revokes the credential.
    issuer.revoke(idx)
    print("issuer revoked credential idx=%d (list version=%d)" % (idx, issuer.version))

    now = 1500                       # still inside the 3600s TTL window
    accepted_stale = v.verify(idx, now)
    print("t=%d verify (post-revoke, within TTL):" % now, accepted_stale)

    now = 1000 + 3600 + 1            # after TTL the cache refreshes -> correct reject
    correct_after_ttl = v.verify(idx, now)
    print("t=%d verify (after TTL refresh):" % now, correct_after_ttl)

    if accepted_stale and not correct_after_ttl:
        print("[SEAM CONFIRMED] revoked credential still verified valid while status list was TTL-cached")
        sys.exit(0)
    print("[fresh status enforced]"); sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
GH6-did-credential-status-staleness-trus.success_criterion: stdout shows pre-revoke verify True, 'issuer revoked ...', post-revoke-within-TTL verify True, after-TTL verify False, then '[SEAM CONFIRMED]' with exit 0 -- proving the verifier accepted a revoked credential because it trusted a stale TTL-cached status list rather than live revocation status.
GH6-did-credential-status-staleness-trus.safety: Local-only simulation of status-list + cache logic; no DID network, issuer endpoint, chain, or keys involved.

GH6-dao-governance-flashstake-snapshot-c.name: DAO governance: flash-borrowed stake captures snapshot vote
GH6-dao-governance-flashstake-snapshot-c.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-dao-governance-flashstake-snapshot-c.setup: Foundry + local in-process EVM ONLY. mkdir flashgov && cd flashgov && forge init --no-git . ; place at test/FlashGov.t.sol ; run: anvil & ; forge test -vv . A governance contract reads voting weight from current balance at vote time (no historical/checkpointed snapshot, no flash-loan guard). A flash-loan stub lends tokens for one transaction; the attacker votes with borrowed weight and repays, leaving zero residual stake.
GH6-dao-governance-flashstake-snapshot-c.success_criterion: forge test -vv passes testFlashStakeVote and logs 'captured YES weight=1000000' with 'attacker residual stake=0', then '[SEAM CONFIRMED]' -- proving a binding governance vote was cast with flash-borrowed weight that the attacker no longer holds, because weight is read from live balance instead of a prior snapshot/checkpoint.
GH6-dao-governance-flashstake-snapshot-c.cleanup: kill the anvil process (pkill -f anvil); rm -rf flashgov/.
GH6-dao-governance-flashstake-snapshot-c.safety: Local-only Foundry in-process EVM; mock token/flash-lender, no mainnet, no real funds or keys.

GH6-erc4337-paymaster-validation-executi.name: ERC-4337 paymaster: validation-phase check invalid at execution
GH6-erc4337-paymaster-validation-executi.authorization: Authorized lab/local-testnet only, signed RoE. Throwaway keys, no mainnet/real funds.
GH6-erc4337-paymaster-validation-executi.setup: Pure-logic simulation of an ERC-4337 EntryPoint flow (validatePaymasterUserOp -> execution -> postOp). Save as aa_paymaster.py and run: python3 aa_paymaster.py . Models a paymaster that approves sponsorship during the validation phase based on a condition (e.g., an allowance/whitelist flag or balance) that the UserOp's own execution then invalidates before postOp, so the paymaster pays for an op it would have rejected.
GH6-erc4337-paymaster-validation-executi.success_criterion: stdout shows 'validation: sponsored (eligible=True)', 'execution done; eligible now=False ; paymaster paid=40', then '[SEAM CONFIRMED] paymaster paid 40 ...' with exit 0 -- proving the paymaster committed funds based on a validation-phase eligibility check that the UserOp's execution invalidated before postOp.
GH6-erc4337-paymaster-validation-executi.safety: Local-only simulation of ERC-4337 EntryPoint phase ordering; no bundler, chain, paymaster deposit, or keys touched.
